title: [InstCombine] Avoid infinite loop in `foldSelectValueEquivalence`
summary:
The generalization extends a pattern from handling only constant equivalence to also supporting cases where the select's true/false value is derived from the compared variable via a simple binary operation. Originally, the optimization replaced `select (x == y), (op x, ...), z` with `select (x == y), (op y, ...), z` only when `op x, ...` was a constant. The patch broadens this to cases where `x` is directly an operand of the true value's instruction, ensuring `y` is simpler (not an instruction) to avoid infinite loops.

This approach transforms a specific constant-folding case into a more general value substitution that works for binary operations like multiplication, addition, etc., as shown in the generalized test. The key insight is to allow substitution if the compared variable is an operand of the true value, while maintaining safety by preventing substitution when it could lead to cyclical replacements. This makes the optimization applicable to a wider range of idioms where a select chooses between a transformed version of a variable and another value based on equality.
